description: Defines virtual fields for a material type. Virtual fields exist only in the query API surface — they expand into real monk3 QueryNode templates when queried. Document block names (e.g. 'root', 'chapter') must match blocks declared in the corresponding mapping.json file.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI for editor validation of virtual mapping documents.

^(?!\\$schema$).*$.description: A document block (e.g. 'root' or a named subdocument type). Each property defines one virtual field.

virtualField.description: A virtual field that does not exist in the search backend. When queried, its 'expansion' QueryNode template is substituted with values from the incoming QueryPayload and translated instead.

type.description: Accepted QueryPayload type for this virtual field. Determines which query payload types the API consumer may pass when querying this field.

expansion.description: A monk3 QueryNode template (same structure as the 'query' field in SearchQueryRequest). String values may contain '{{data}}' as a whole-string placeholder, replaced at query time with the entire incoming QueryPayload JSON object. Only whole-string placeholders are supported (e.g. "{{data}}", not "prefix-{{data}}"). Expansion templates may only reference real mapped fields — not other virtual fields.
